protocol.name: wlr_layer_shell_unstable_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: zwlr_layer_shell_v1

description.summary: create surfaces that are layers of the desktop

interface.description: Clients can use this interface to assign the surface_layer role to wl_surfaces. Such surfaces are displayed between the background and applications.

request.name: get_layer_surface

description.summary: create a layer_surface from a surface

request.description: Create a layer surface for an existing surface. This assigns the role of layer_surface, or raises a protocol error if another role is already assigned.

arg.name: output
arg.summary: Restrict to a specific output, or NULL to use default

arg.name: layer
arg.summary: Layer to add this surface to

request.name: destroy
request.description: This request indicates that the client will not use the layer_shell object any more. Objects that have been created through this instance are not affected.

entry.name: already_constructed
entry.summary: wl_surface has a buffer attached or committed

enum.name: layer
enum.description: These values indicate which layers a surface can be rendered to. They are ordered by z depth, bottom-most first. Traditional shell surfaces will typically be rendered between the bottom and top layers. Fullscreen shell surfaces are typically rendered at the top layer. Multiple surfaces can share a single layer, and the ordering within a single layer is undefined.

interface.name: zwlr_layer_surface_v1
interface.description: An interface that may be implemented by a wl_surface, for surfaces that are designed to be rendered as a layer of a stacked desktop-like environment.

request.name: set_size

description.summary: sets the size of the surface

request.description:
Sets the size of the surface in surface-local coordinates. The compositor will display the surface centered with respect to its anchors.
If you pass 0 for either value, the compositor will assign it and inform you of the assignment in the configure event. You must set your anchor to opposite edges in the axis the compositor assigns. If you do not, the compositor will trap you with an invalid_size error. When the width or height is 0 and the corresponding horizontal or vertical anchors are set, or when a corresponding anchor is not set, the compositor treats this as a hint to expand the surface.

request.name: set_anchor

description.summary: configures the anchor point of the surface

request.description: Requests that the compositor anchor the surface to the specified edges and corners. If two orthogonal edges are specified, e.g. 'top' and 'left', the anchor point will be the top left corner of the output; otherwise, the anchor point will be centered on that edge, or the center if none is specified.

request.name: set_exclusive_zone

description.summary: configures the exclusive geometry of this surface

request.description:
Requests that the compositor avoids occluding an area with other surfaces. The compositor's use of this information is implementation-dependent - do not assume that this region will be vacant from other surfaces.
A value of -1 indicates that the surface does not wish to be moved to accommodate for other surfaces, and the compositor should extend it to the edges of the output, ignoring the position set by set_margin.

request.name: set_margin

description.summary: sets a margin from the anchor point

request.description: Requests that the surface be placed some distance away from the anchor point on the output, in surface-local coordinates. Setting this value for edges you are not anchored to has no effect.

request.name: set_keyboard_interactivity
request.description: Set how keyboard events are delivered to this surface. By default, layer shell surfaces do not receive keyboard events; this request can be used to change this.

request.name: get_popup

description.summary: assign this layer_surface as an xdg_popup parent

request.description: This assigns an xdg_popup's parent to this layer_surface. This popup should have been created via xdg_surface::get_popup with the parent set to NULL, and this request must be invoked before committing the popup's initial state.

request.name: ack_configure
request.description: When a configure event is received, if a client does not need to change the state of the surface in response to the configure event, then the client must nonetheless send an ack_configure request before committing a new surface.

arg.name: serial
arg.summary: the serial from the configure event

request.name: destroy
request.description: This request destroys the layer surface.

request.name: set_layer

description.summary: change the layer of the surface

request.description: Change the layer that the surface is rendered on. Layer is specified by the corresponding value in the zwlr_layer_shell_v1 enum.

arg.name: layer
arg.summary: layer to move this surface to

request.name: set_exclusive_edge

description.summary: set the edge the exclusive zone will be applied to

request.description: Requests an edge for the exclusive zone to apply. The exclusive edge will be automatically deduced from anchor points when possible, but when two orthogonal edges are anchored, the result is ambiguous and the compositor would be expected to set the exclusive zone to 0. This request solves that ambiguity.

event.name: configure
event.description: The configure event asks the client to resize its surface.

event.name: closed
event.description: The closed event is sent by the compositor when the surface will no longer be shown. The output may have been destroyed or the user may have asked for it to be removed. Further changes to the surface will be ignored. The client should destroy the resource after receiving this event, and create a new surface if they so choose.

enum.name: keyboard_interactivity

description.summary: types of keyboard interaction possible for a layer shell surface

enum.description: Types of keyboard interaction possible for layer shell surfaces. The rationale for this is that some surfaces (cursors, tooltips) should not be able to receive keyboard focus while others (a lock screen) should always have such focus.

entry.name: exclusive
entry.summary: request exclusive keyboard focus if this surface is above the shell surface layer

entry.name: on_demand
entry.summary: this surface can be focused and unfocused by the user

entry.name: top
entry.summary: the top edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: bottom
entry.summary: the bottom edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: left
entry.summary: the left edge of the anchor rectangle

entry.name: right
entry.summary: the right edge of the anchor rectangle
